questions.statement: Which of the following is NOT included in a progressive and child-centered education?
options: Use of digital technology and teaching-learning materials /
Emphasising learning from activities, experiences, and playful exploratory manner /
Emphasis only on course completion with structured curriculum /
Active participation of students with democratic and free expression /
The importance of continuity in behavioural methods /
The difference between what a child can currently do compared to their long-term potential /
The difference between what a child can do on their own, and what they can do with the help of a more knowledgeable other /
An area of research that is dedicated to developing consistent and useful learning and teaching materials&nbsp; /

questions.statement: Individuals reason that- &lsquo;Right action is defined by self-chosen ethical principles of conscience&rsquo; in Kolhberg&rsquo;s stage of-
options: Good girl-good boy orientation /
Social contract orientation&nbsp; /
Social order maintaining orientation&nbsp; /
Universal ethical principles /
A and D only /
B and C only /
B and D only /
C and D only /

questions.statement: There are many types of students in your school. Some are talented, some are creative, and some are very slow children. What would be the best educational programs for talented children ?
options: Allow them to do any work in the school and not give it to other students.&nbsp; /
Motivate them to work up to the minimum demands of education and give the same or any precious item as a gift.&nbsp; /
Develop thinking skills in them and give them opportunities to engage in different ideas.&nbsp; /
All of the above.&nbsp; /

questions.statement: The concept of &lsquo;Inclusive Education&rsquo; as advocated in the Right to Education Act, 2009 is based on
options: the behaviouristic principles.&nbsp;&nbsp; /
a sympathetic attitude towards disabled.&nbsp;&nbsp; /
a right-based humanistic perspective.&nbsp;&nbsp; /
mainstreaming of the disabled by offering them primarily vocational education.&nbsp; /

questions.statement: To enable the student to make conceptual changes in their thinking, a teacher should
options: discourage children from thinking on their own and ask them to just listen to her and follow that /
offer an explanation in a lecture mode /
make clear and convincing explanations and have discussions with the students /
offer rewards for children who change their thinking /

questions.statement: Individual differences in a progressive classroom should be treated as
options: a failure on the part of teacher. /
criteria for making ability-based groups. /
important for planning of teaching-learning process. /
a hindrance to the process of learning. /

questions.statement: Formative assessment is typically conducted during
options: the development or improvement of a programme /
feedback session /
performance measurement /
comparison between the performance of two students /

questions.statement: In order to address gender stereotypes in the classroom, it is important to&nbsp;
options: Make cross-gender interactions restricted&nbsp; /
avoid discussions around gender discrimination /
Present examples that challenge those stereotypes /
Practice gender bias through everyday practices /
